metadata.notes: agy can cancel a turn (STEP_STATUS_INTERRUPTED / 'cancelled by user' in binary) but no interrupt marker reaches the transcript (all lines status:DONE) and the parser has no IsUserInterrupt path for antigravity. daemon=incapable → frozen. Matches codex 2.20 (incapable); driver also stubs interrupt.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready (n/a — frozen; the driver also stubs interrupt)

Route: frozen (an ESC interrupt is not observable for antigravity).

## What the scenario pins

`ready → working → ready`; state does not stick in `working` post-interrupt; an interrupt is observed.

## agent_supports: yes

The agy CLI can cancel an in-flight turn — the binary carries the cancel/interrupt vocabulary (`STEP_STATUS_INTERRUPTED`, `STEP_STATUS_CANCELED`, `canceled by user` / `cancelled by user`, `AskQuestion cancelled by user`). So ESC/cancel is a real agent capability.

## daemon_capability: incapable

The canonical claudecode/gemini-cli cells observe the interrupt via a transcript marker: claudecode's parser.recordUserInterruptFlags fires on the `[Request interrupted by user]` line, and gemini-cli writes an equivalent marker its parser detects. Antigravity emits NO such marker: (1) the parser has no IsUserInterrupt path — parser.go models only USER_INPUT→working, PLANNER_RESPONSE→working/ready, and tool results; there is no interrupt-marker branch. (2) The interrupt is never persisted: every line in BOTH transcript.jsonl and transcript_full.jsonl is status:"DONE" (verified across all real conversations), and no `[Request interrupted...]` / `cancelled by user` text appears in any recorded transcript — the cancel is an internal IPC state (STEP_STATUS_INTERRUPTED) that is never appended. With no marker, an interrupted turn simply has no terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE, so the daemon cannot distinguish 'cancelled' from 'still working'. No trace exists → the working→ready interrupt edge cannot be observed. This matches the codex 2.20 verdict (daemon=incapable, frozen).

## driver_capability: n/a

Moot under a frozen route — and additionally the driver-interactive.sh `interrupt` arm is a not_implemented stub (DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"), so even were the daemon capable the cell would be driver=gap:interrupt.
caveats: Re-assess if Antigravity ever persists an interrupt/cancel marker (or a STEP_STATUS_INTERRUPTED step) to the transcript, AND the adapter gains an IsUserInterrupt path. Both are required to observe the working→ready interrupt edge.

sources.note: no IsUserInterrupt / interrupt-marker branch; an interrupted turn just lacks its terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE
100% status:"DONE"; no '[Request interrupted...]' / 'cancelled by user' text — the cancel is never persisted
binary strings: STEP_STATUS_INTERRUPTED / 'cancelled by user' confirm the agent can cancel, but the marker stays internal
interrupt arm is not_implemented (DRIVE_ELICITS omits it) — secondary driver gap
